entries.original_incomplete_question: Alice has a deck of  $[BLANK]$  cards,  $5$  suits of  $8$  cards each. She picks any  $20$  cards and gives the rest to Bob. Now each turn Alice picks any of her cards and lays it face-up onto the table, then Bob similarly picks any of his cards and lays it face-up onto the table. If this pair of cards has the same suit or the same value, Bob gains a point. What is the maximum number of points he can guarantee regardless of Alice’s actions?

variants.variant: Alice possesses a total of $[BLANK]$ cards, consisting of $5$ suits with $8$ cards in each. She selects $20$ cards for herself and hands the remaining cards to Bob. During each round, Alice chooses one of her cards and places it face-up on the table, followed by Bob who also selects and places one of his cards face-up. If the cards match in suit or value, Bob scores a point. What is the highest number of points Bob can secure no matter how Alice plays?

Alice has $[BLANK]$ cards in her possession, divided into $5$ suits with $8$ cards per suit. She draws $20$ cards for herself and passes the rest to Bob. In each turn, Alice takes one of her cards and places it face-up on the table, then Bob does the same with one of his cards. If their chosen cards share either the same suit or value, Bob earns a point. What is the maximum number of points Bob can ensure, regardless of Alice’s choices?

Alice owns $[BLANK]$ cards, organized into $5$ different suits, each containing $8$ cards. She randomly selects $20$ cards for herself, giving the leftover cards to Bob. Each turn, Alice reveals one of her cards face-up on the table, after which Bob reveals one of his own. Bob will score a point if the two cards are of the same suit or value. What is the maximum number of points Bob can guarantee irrespective of Alice's moves?

What is the maximum number of points Bob can guarantee no matter how Alice plays her cards?

In each round, Alice places one of her cards face-up on the table, and then Bob places one of his cards face-up as well. If their cards share either the same suit or value, Bob earns a point. What is the highest number of points Bob can assure himself, regardless of Alice's decisions?
